title: Another significant symbol in this story is the “Worn Path” because it represent
content:
Another significant symbol in this story is the “Worn Path” because it represents the literal and metaphorical hardships of Phoenix’s life and journey. This path represents her struggles in her past with the symbols of thorn bushes and logs. Phoenix is driven by love and duty despite her age and exhaustion, which is illustrated in this part of the story: “Seems like there are chains about my feet, time I get this far.”. It is in her nature to sacrifice, as she has done before, and she will do it again. Her journey is a testament to her strength, heart, selflessness, and determination. A worn path is an important representation of love and endurance for those who heroically and quietly dedicate themselves to others.

from Week 5 Discussion
Feb 3, 2025 7:11PM
Ashley Kenny
Hi Vielka, I like your analysis above. Especially the one of the black dogs in Eudora Welty’s A Worn Path, which reflects Phoenix Jackson’s perseverance and sacrifice. A way I interpreted the black dog is as a representation of racial and social struggles. Phoenix is in the Jim Crow time period of the South, and faces racial obstacles that are sudden and unexpected, much like the dog. The man who helped her is assumed to be superior because of her old age and race, and he pointed a gun at her for his own amusement because she is less than. To add on to your discussion, I would look into the internal struggles and resilience of phoenix, symbolized by the landscape. for example, the thorn bush, Phoenix states, “I in the thorny bush,” and she is determined in a way that she acknowledges and understands her challenges and keeps moving without complaints. Welty symbolism represents perseverance, the love in her sacrifice, and her journey, both literal and metaphorical.
